column1: # Rows with mp_id=NO_MP_ENTRY are literature-validated but EXCLUDED from GNN metrics (no MP structure to ingest).

column9: Fig.1(a) read-off: cycles 1-200 discharge slopes through ~2.6 V at mid-capacity (1.5-4.5 V window); no numeric V_avg stated anywhere in text (full PDF fetched from Caltech authors library and searched)
column10: coin-type vs Na metal; 1 M NaPF6 EC/PC 1:1; C/20; 1.5-4.5 V
column12: EXCLUDED (operator-validated 2026-06-09: PHASE-IDENTITY MISMATCH; supersedes prior 'CONFIRMED Tier C LOW CONFIDENCE')
column13: OPERATOR VALIDATION 2026-06-09: ~2.6 V read-off confirmed accurate, but it is the operating voltage of the amorphous NaxFePO4/a-FePO4 phase formed by irreversible first-charge amorphization (XRD/EXAFS/TEM) — NOT crystalline maricite, which is the MP structure (mp-19226) the GNN ingested. Row EXCLUDED from canonical metrics for PHASE-IDENTITY MISMATCH (stronger reason than the prior Tier C read-off flag); canonical headline metrics = the n=6 'without Tier C' block (raw MAE 0.668 V / LOOCV 0.802 V / upper CI 1.092 V), verdict tier unchanged. Original notes retained: lattice-verified maricite: MP 5.00/6.82/8.94 A vs lit 5.05/6.86/8.99 A (olivine=mp-1192859 excluded). Cross-source: Mohsin 2023 (10.1155/2023/6054452, KIT OA PDF) text-anchored 'distinct plateau region ... at 2.6 V' for milled/coated m-NaFePO4 agrees within 0.05 V. Proposal mp-755097 was WRONG (=Fe6O5F7).

column9: "The redox potential of each phase was similar to 3.4 V (vs Li) for the Li-ion cell and similar to 3.2 V (vs Na) for the Na-ion cell" (abstract, OpenAlex record)
column10: Na-ion cell vs Na; 129 mAh/g (one-electron per Fe)
column13: Proposal citation WRONG: 'Chem.Mater. 25,3614 / 10.1021/cm4014104' does not resolve on Crossref; true primary = Kim et al. JACS 134,10369-10372 (2012) 10.1021/ja3038646. Proposal's in-training flag (mp-754874) VERIFIED FALSE: training graph battery-id mp-754874_Na is the Na0-1Cr3O8 pair (na_ion_candidates.csv) — mp-id namespace collision; NFPP is genuinely held-out. Lattice-verified vs Kim 2012 Pn21a (6.61/10.81/18.44 vs 6.53/10.65/18.08 A). MP e_hull=0.196 eV/atom (high for an experimental phase) noted.

column9: "the beta- and ABW-phases show monotonously increasing voltage profiles with the average voltages of ~ 4.3 and 4.5 V vs. Na+/Na0, respectively" (OSTI accepted manuscript, Sec. 3.5)
column10: coin vs Na metal; EC:PC:DMC 45:45:10 + 2% FEC; C/25; 2.5-4.8 V
column13: FIRST-CHARGE average; subsequent discharge delivers only ~11% of 180 mAh/g charge capacity — treat as upper-bound-like, kinetically polarized value. Rietveld lattice match: Chiring a=5.231/b=10.008/c=7.386 beta=90.16 vs MP 5.299/7.476/10.138 beta=90.2 (b/c setting swap). STEP-2 OVERLAP ROW: V_MP=3.961 (battery pair mp-540093->mp-562796), prior V_pred=3.727. Proposal's 'maricite Pmnb, Whittingham 2011, ~4.5 V' citation REPLACED: maricite alpha-NaCoPO4 is electrochemically inactive (<10 mAh/g) per same paper.

column9: same snippet as ABW row: "average voltages of ~ 4.3 and 4.5 V vs. Na+/Na0" — beta=4.3 (body text/Fig.7); abstract+conclusion round beta to ~4.2 (0.1 V internal variance recorded)
column10: coin vs Na metal; EC:PC:DMC 45:45:10 + 2% FEC; C/25; 2.5-4.8 V
column13: FIRST-CHARGE average; ~11% reversible. Rietveld lattice match: a=10.164 c=23.854 (P65) vs MP 10.263/24.094. STEP-2 OVERLAP ROW: V_MP=3.7625 (battery pair mp-850025->mp-683773), prior V_pred=3.9209. Gutierrez 2017 'Red'-phase (10.1021/acsami.6b14341) is a DIFFERENT polymorph (P21/c, trigonal-bipyramidal Co) — not used.

column9: "delivers initial discharge capacity of 110 mAh g-1 at a rate of 1/20 C (6.2 mA g-1) with well-defined voltage plateaus at 3.06 and 2.91 V vs. Na metal" (TUS Pure abstract); V_avg=(3.06+2.91)/2=2.985 (two-step Na2->Na1.5->Na1, plateaus of ~equal capacity)

column13: Na-specific paper per operator decision #3: Kawabe/Yabuuchi/Komaba ECom 13,1225 (2011) replaces Ellis 2007 Nat Mater (Li-cycled; its 3.5 V is the Li value). Polymorph matches Ellis 2007 Pbcn: MP 5.281/11.954/14.042 vs lit 5.220/13.854/11.774 (axis-setting swap). Equal-capacity-split assumption is standard for the two Na sites; recorded as Tier B arithmetic.

column9: "one can reversibly remove two sodium ions per formula unit via two-step redox plateaus of equal amplitudes centered at ~3.7 and ~4.2 V vs. Na+/Na0" (open-access full text); V_avg=(3.7+4.2)/2=3.95
column10: half-cell vs Na metal; 1 M NaPF6/PC; 128 mAh/g
column13: Anchored to Bianchini/Tarascon NComms 10,585 (2019) description of pristine NVPF (their baseline), replacing proposal's structural-only Le Meins 1999 citation. Shakoor 2012 J.Mater.Chem. exists but its abstract carries no numeric V and proposal's DOI for it (c2jm35608b) was wrong (true: 10.1039/c2jm33862a). MP mp-694937 is the only NVPF entry; a=9.167~b=9.198, c=10.924 vs lit ~9.03/9.04/10.75 (+1.5% DFT) — polymorph-matched.

column9: Bo/Ceder ChemMater 28,1419 (2016) C/50 vs Na+/Na: O3->O'3 segment "between 2.6 and 3.1 V" (Na1.0->0.75); P'3 region "between 3.1 and 3.75 V" (Na0.73->0.38); reversibility limit ~3.7 V at Na0.4. V_avg(charge,C/50, reversible 0.6 Na)=(0.25*2.85+0.35*3.43)/0.60=3.19~3.2; Fig.1 read-off 3.2-3.3 consistent
column10: Swagelok vs Na metal; 1 M NaPF6 EC/DEC 1:1; C/50
column13: Discovery citation Komaba 2010 ECom 12,355 (10.1016/j.elecom.2009.12.033 — DOI verified via Unpaywall; full text inaccessible this run) retained for provenance; V anchored to Bo/Ceder measured near-equilibrium curves. PROTOCOL SENSITIVITY: common 2.5-3.6 V cycling yields ~2.9-3.0 V discharge average — 3.2 V is the charge-branch average over the full reversible range; flagged. Proposal mp-19427 was WRONG (=Na7(CoO3)2); R-3m rhombohedral cell verified.

column9: "delivering a reversible capacity of 82 mAh g-1 with the Fe3+/Fe2+ redox potential centered around 3 V (vs. Na/Na+)" (Semantic Scholar abstract record)
